sentence: Farmer let us stay in his barn tonight.
A farmer allowed us to spend the night in his barn.
Tonight a farmer let us shelter in his barn.
There’d be another Conductor to take us on tonight, they said.
They told us another Conductor would be coming to take us on that night.
Another Conductor was set to take us onward tonight, they said.
We made it to Albany.
We managed to reach Albany.
Albany — we finally made it there.

After the American Revolution, her mama and papa moved up from New York.
I can’t believe it, but she’s in Toronto, staying with Missus Blunt.

She's in Toronto staying with Missus Blunt — I still can't believe it.

I wonder what is going on with everyone back at our plantation.

She was white, so I thought she'd be cross with us for being so uppity, but she welcomed us as nicely as could be and went off to fetch Mister Frost.
I’ll see the ocean!
I am going to see the ocean!
The ocean — I'll actually get to see it!

Papa ought to be in Toronto by now.
By now, Papa should have arrived in Toronto.

Today the man's wife brought new clothes out to the barn for all of us, so we would look respectable on arriving in Canada.
The man's wife came out to the barn with fresh clothes for everyone, wanting us to look respectable when we reached Canada.

They mostly call us colored up here in the north, not Negroes.
Up here in the north they mostly call us colored, not Negroes.
In the north, colored is what they mostly call us, rather than Negroes.

For the first time, we are celebrating Christmas as free folk.

When he ran away she wouldn’t go with him.
